ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"build_scheduler","new":"","old":"","op":"replace_function","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = max(1, TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100)
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=total_steps)","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Use the fixed-step budget more effectively by enabling a cosine schedule over the actual run length, while slightly increasing model capacity and switching to AdamW for better CIFAR-10 optimization.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Use more data per fixed-step run by doubling the batch size, then slightly raise the AdamW step size and relax decay so the model can keep fitting under the existing cosine schedule."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Increase representational depth now that the fixed-step optimizer setup is stable; a fourth pooling block should let the CNN extract stronger CIFAR-10 features without changing the rest of the training loop."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 4

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Spend more of the fixed-step budget on real CIFAR-10 examples by raising the batch size again, and slightly increase the AdamW step size so the larger-batch updates still make progress."}

Widening the feature stack and classifier should let the model fit CIFAR-10 a bit better without changing the training loop."}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Push the fixed-step regime closer to a full CIFAR-10 pass by increasing the batch again, and raise the OneCycle peak slightly so the larger-batch updates stay sufficiently aggressive."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The larger model still looks a bit underfit, and the last gain came from relaxing decay, so reduce AdamW regularization one more notch while keeping the best-performing schedule and batch setup."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The trend so far says the model still benefits from less regularization, and there is no sign of overfitting within this short fixed-step run, so remove the remaining AdamW decay and let the network fit a bit more tightly."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The model appears to be near an optimization plateau, so nudge the OneCycle peak higher to keep the larger batch and wider network moving a bit harder through the remaining budget."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The larger batch and zero decay setup is still improving when the peak LR rises, so push the OneCycle maximum a bit higher to keep squeezing progress out of the remaining steps."}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Restore the peak learning rate that had been working best, then make AdamW a bit more responsive by lowering the second-moment beta for this short one-cycle run."}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The short one-cycle run is still responsive to optimizer dynamics, so reduce AdamW's second-moment inertia a bit further to let the final steps adapt faster."}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.92)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The last gain came from making AdamW more reactive, so continue that direction with a slightly lower second-moment beta to help the last few steps adapt faster."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.92)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"Bring AdamW back to the better-reacting beta2 setting and try a slightly hotter peak LR; the last stable gains came from stronger updates, so this is the remaining nearby tuning axis."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The current AdamW betas are already tuned for responsiveness, so the remaining scale lever is a small peak-LR bump to see whether the larger model can still squeeze out a bit more from the last budget."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The last LR bump overshot the best run, so revert to the 2.3e-3 peak that previously gave the lowest validation loss."}
